description: Cycads are ancient gymnosperm plants — living fossils with a lineage stretching back over 300 million years. Today's species belong to three families: Cycadaceae (Cycas), Zamiaceae (Encephalartos, Zamia, Ceratozamia, Macrozamia, Dioon and others) and Stangeriaceae (Stangeria, Bowenia). Many species are critically threatened in the wild and are subject to CITES Appendix I or II protections. Slow-growing and long-lived, cycads are prized collectors' plants.
